key_insight: PBE-trained machine-learning interatomic potentials can wrongly predict HfO2’s ground state, overstabilizing a low-density I41/amd phase instead of the experimentally observed monoclinic P21/c structure.
engineering_relevance: This shows that ML-based crystal and phase-transition predictions can inherit exchange-correlation functional errors, affecting materials screening and ferroelectric pathway design.
method: Compared PBE-based MLIP predictions against known HfO2 phase stability.
Benchmarked against different DFT functionals to trace the source of the error.
Examined ferroelectric switching paths and energy barriers under large lattice relaxations.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that multiple PBE-based models, including NequIP-OAM-L and MatterSim-v1-5M, reproduce the same incorrect phase ordering seen from PBE calculations.
watch_item: Do not assume a PBE-trained MLIP is reliable for phase stability or barrier predictions when low-density sixfold-coordinated structures are involved.
paper_title: A wrong ground-state structure of HfO$_2$ predicted by machine-learning interatomic potentials based on the PBE functional
